name: USS Anthedon (AS 24)
events: 5 Dec 1944 At 0830 H/5, USS Hardhead (T/Cdr. F.A. Greenup, USN) ended her 2nd war patrol at Fremantle.
She was escorted in (identity of the escort not known). The escort had joined around 0500 H/5.
Before entering harbour noise and D/G trails were carried out.
At Fremantle she was taken in hand for refit by a relief crew from Submarine Division 261 and by USS Anthedon (T/Cdr. R.E. Hawes, USN).

11 Apr 1945 Around 1230 I/11, USS Hardhead (T/Cdr. F.A. Greenup, USN, with T/R.Adm. J. Fife, USN on board) enters Subic Bay in the Philippines to reload torpedoes, stores and fuel. She was also taken in hand for some repairs by USS Anthedon (T/Capt. R.E. Hawes, USN).
USS Hardhead was escorted in by USS Jaccard (Lt.Cdr. C.R. Hamilton, USNR) which had joined at 0715 I/11.
At Subic Bay, T/R.Adm. Fife left the submarine.

10 Jun 1945 At 0852 H/10, USS Icefish (T/Cdr. R.W. Peterson, USN) arrived at Subic Bay where she moored alongside USS Anthedon (T/Capt. R.E. Hawes, USN). USS Icefish was escorted in by USS Johnnie Hutchins (Lt.Cdr. H.M. Godsey, USNR) by which had joined around 0430 H/10.
The survivors were landed and USS Icefish fuelled. Also some minor repairs were made. (2)
18 Jun 1945 USS Hawkbill (T/Cdr. W. Scanland, Jr., USN) ended her fourth war patrol at Subic Bay, Philippines.
She was escorted in USS Johnnie Hutchins (Lt.Cdr. H.M. Godsey, USNR) by which had joined around 0500 I/18.
USS Hawkbill was then taken in hand for refit by USS Anthedon (T/Capt. R.E. Hawes, USN).
25 Jul 1945 At 0530 I/25, USS Hawkbill (T/Cdr. W. Scanland, Jr., USN) was joined by USS Pratt (Cdr. R.H. Wanless, USNR) which then escorted her to Subic Bay.

At 0943 I/25, USS Hawkbill moored alongside USS Anthedon (T/Capt. R.E. Hawes, USN) and was taken in hand for the most essential repairs to the battle damage that had been sustained. Also the submarine was fuelled and received nine replacement torpedoes. (3)
11 Aug 1945 At 1100 I/11, USS Hardhead (T/Lt.Cdr. J.L. Haines, USN) ended her 6th war patrol at Subic Bay.
She was escorted in by USS Woodson (Lt.Cdr. A.R. Corlett, USNR) which had joined around 0600 I/11.
At Subic Bay, USS Hardhead was taken in hand for refit by USS Anthedon (T/.Capt. R.E. Hawes, USN).
